subtasks.description: Wire severity classifier and evidence sanitizer into documentation pipeline before report generation
subtasks.details: Import severity.py (task 17) and sanitizer.py (task 16) modules. After dedup check passes, call sanitizer.sanitize_evidence() on all evidence fields (response_text, tool_calls, logs). Store sanitized versions in separate fields for report generation. Call severity.classify_severity() with finding context to get severity rating (P0-P3) and confidence score. Store severity and confidence in finding record. Validate severity output matches expected enum ['P0', 'P1', 'P2', 'P3']. Log sanitization metrics (redaction count) and severity classification results.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: provide finding with JWT token in evidence and tool_misuse category. Assert sanitizer replaces JWT with '[JWT_REDACTED]'. Assert severity classifier returns P1-P3 rating. Mock both dependencies and verify they're called with correct arguments.

subtasks.title: Implement Anthropic API call for structured report drafting with quality validation
subtasks.description: Use Claude Sonnet to generate professional vulnerability reports with structured output validation
subtasks.details: Load prompts/doc.md template. Construct prompt with finding context: category, severity, sanitized evidence, attack payload, verdict rationale. Call Anthropic API (Claude Sonnet 4.5) with structured output schema: {title: str, description: str, clinical_impact: str, reproduction_steps: list[str], observed_behavior: str, expected_behavior: str, remediation_suggestions: list[str], validation_steps: list[str]}. Use temperature=0.3 for consistency. Validate response against schema using Pydantic models. Retry up to 2 times if validation fails. Store raw LLM response and parsed structured output. Calculate response quality score based on field completeness and length thresholds (description >100 chars, clinical_impact >50 chars, remediation_suggestions >2 items). Log token usage and cost to agent_steps.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test with mocked Anthropic API. Provide VerdictRecord with pass verdict, P2 severity, sanitized evidence. Mock API returns valid structured output. Assert parsed report contains all required fields. Test retry logic: mock API returns invalid JSON first attempt, valid on second. Assert successful parsing after retry.

subtasks.title: Implement Jinja2 template rendering with all metadata
subtasks.description: Render professional markdown vulnerability reports using templates/finding.md.j2 with complete metadata
subtasks.details: Create templates/finding.md.j2 with sections: Title, Summary, Clinical Impact, Severity, OWASP/MITRE/HIPAA Mappings, Reproduction Steps, Observed Behavior, Expected Behavior, Remediation, Validation Steps, Metadata (timestamp, session_id, attack_id, finding_id). Load LLM-generated structured output from step 3. Enrich with metadata: severity rating, confidence score, OWASP mapping (category -> OWASP-LLM01-LLM10), MITRE ATT&CK IDs (hardcoded mappings), HIPAA sections (164.308, 164.312 based on category). Render template using Jinja2 Environment. Validate rendered markdown has all sections populated (no empty {{ }} tags). Write rendered output to findings.report_markdown field. Store rendering timestamp.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: provide structured report output from LLM with all fields populated. Render template with metadata (severity=P2, category=prompt-injection-indirect). Assert rendered markdown contains '[P2]' tag, 'OWASP-LLM01' reference, reproduction steps formatted as numbered list. Assert no Jinja2 template variables remain unreplaced.

subtasks.title: Implement HITL routing decision tree for P0/P1 and low-confidence
subtasks.description: Route high-stakes findings to human approval queue instead of auto-filing to GitLab
subtasks.details: Implement routing logic: if severity in ['P0', 'P1'] OR confidence < 0.7, route to human approval. Insert record into human_approval_queue table with fields: finding_id, severity, confidence, title (from LLM output), status='pending', queued_at=now(), reason (e.g., 'High severity: P0' or 'Low confidence: 0.65'). Skip GitLab filing for queued findings. Log routing decision with rationale. For findings passing criteria (P2/P3 AND confidence >= 0.7), continue to GitLab filing step. Return routing result: {routed_to_queue: bool, queue_id: int|null, reason: str|null}.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: Test P0 severity with confidence=0.9, assert routed to queue. Test P2 severity with confidence=0.6, assert routed to queue. Test P2 severity with confidence=0.85, assert NOT routed (continues to GitLab). Query human_approval_queue table, verify record inserted with correct reason field.

subtasks.title: Implement GitLab API integration with issue creation and label attachment plus comprehensive tests
subtasks.description: File vulnerability reports as GitLab issues with structured labels and capture issue URLs
subtasks.details: Implement GitLab API client using requests library. Read GITLAB_TOKEN and GITLAB_PROJECT_ID from environment. Construct issue payload: {title: LLM title, description: rendered markdown from step 4, labels: [f'severity-{severity}', f'category-{category}', f'OWASP-{owasp_id}', f'MITRE-{mitre_id}', f'HIPAA-{hipaa_section}'], confidential: true for P0/P1}. POST to /api/v4/projects/{project_id}/issues. Parse response, extract issue.iid and issue.web_url. Update findings.gitlab_issue_id = issue.iid, findings.gitlab_issue_url = issue.web_url. Handle API errors: 401 (auth), 404 (project not found), 422 (validation), 429 (rate limit). Retry with exponential backoff for 429/5xx errors (max 3 attempts). Log API call to agent_steps with cost tracking. Write comprehensive integration tests with mocked GitLab API covering success, auth failure, rate limit retry, and payload validation scenarios.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test with mocked GitLab API: Provide complete finding with P2 severity, confidence=0.85 (should bypass queue). Mock POST /api/v4/projects/123/issues returns {iid: 456, web_url: 'https://gitlab.com/proj/issues/456'}. Assert findings.gitlab_issue_id=456, findings.gitlab_issue_url populated. Verify labels array contains 'severity-P2', 'category-prompt-injection-indirect', OWASP/MITRE/HIPAA tags. Test 429 rate limit: mock returns 429 first call, 201 second call, assert retry succeeds.

tasks.title: Implement human_approval_queue CLI workflow
tasks.description: Enable operator review and approval of high-stakes findings
tasks.details: Extend cli.py with Typer commands. `agentforge-redteam queue list`: query human_approval_queue WHERE status='pending', display table: queue_id, finding_id, severity, title, confidence, created_at. `agentforge-redteam queue approve <queue_id>`: update status='approved', reviewed_by=os.getenv('USER'), reviewed_at=now(). Call GitLab API to file issue (same logic as task 43). Update findings.gitlab_issue_id. Print 'Approved and filed: <issue_url>'. `agentforge-redteam queue reject <queue_id> --reason <text>`: update status='rejected', write rejection_reason. If rejected verdict, convert to new ground-truth case: copy attack_id + verdict_id to evals/judge_ground_truth/<category>/rejected_<queue_id>.yaml with notes='Human-rejected. Reason: {{reason}}'. Print 'Rejected and added to ground-truth.' Test: seed queue, run approve, verify issue filed.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: seed human_approval_queue with 1 pending finding. Run `queue list`, verify output. Run `queue approve <id>`, verify GitLab API called (mocked). Query findings table, assert gitlab_issue_id set.

subtasks.title: Implement 'queue list' command with formatted table display
subtasks.description: Create Typer command to query and display pending approval queue items in a formatted table
subtasks.details: Add 'queue list' command to cli.py using Typer. Query human_approval_queue table WHERE status='pending'. Fetch columns: queue_id, finding_id, severity, title, confidence, created_at. Use rich.table or tabulate library to display formatted table output. Sort by created_at DESC (newest first). Handle empty queue gracefully with message 'No pending approvals'. Include error handling for database connection issues.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: seed human_approval_queue with 3 pending entries. Run 'agentforge-redteam queue list', capture stdout, assert table contains all 3 entries with correct columns. Test empty queue returns friendly message.

subtasks.title: Implement 'queue approve' command with GitLab filing and database updates
subtasks.description: Create approval command that updates queue status, files GitLab issue, and links finding to issue
subtasks.details: Add 'queue approve <queue_id>' command to cli.py. Validate queue_id exists and status='pending'. Update human_approval_queue: set status='approved', reviewed_by=os.getenv('USER'), reviewed_at=datetime.now(). Reuse GitLab issue filing logic from task 43 (python-gitlab API). Fetch finding details from findings table via finding_id. Create issue with template from templates/finding.md.j2. Update findings.gitlab_issue_id with returned issue IID. Print success message: 'Approved and filed: <issue_url>'. Wrap in transaction for atomicity. Handle GitLab API errors (network, auth) gracefully.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: seed queue with 1 pending finding. Mock GitLab API client. Run 'queue approve <id>', verify human_approval_queue.status='approved', findings.gitlab_issue_id set, mocked API create_issue called with correct payload. Assert output contains issue URL.

subtasks.title: Implement 'queue reject' command with rejection reason and ground-truth conversion
subtasks.description: Create rejection command that records reason and generates ground-truth test case for rejected verdicts
subtasks.details: Add 'queue reject <queue_id> --reason <text>' command to cli.py. Validate queue_id exists and status='pending', reason is non-empty. Update human_approval_queue: set status='rejected', rejection_reason=reason, reviewed_by=os.getenv('USER'), reviewed_at=datetime.now(). Fetch finding + verdict + attack_id from database. Convert to ground-truth case: create evals/judge_ground_truth/<category>/rejected_<queue_id>.yaml. YAML structure: {attack_id: <id>, verdict_id: <id>, expected_outcome: 'reject', notes: 'Human-rejected. Reason: {{reason}}', reviewed_by: <user>, reviewed_at: <timestamp>}. Ensure evals/judge_ground_truth/<category>/ directory exists (create if needed). Print: 'Rejected and added to ground-truth: evals/judge_ground_truth/<category>/rejected_<queue_id>.yaml'.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: seed queue with 1 pending finding linked to verdict. Run 'queue reject <id> --reason "False positive - benign behavior"', verify human_approval_queue.status='rejected', YAML file created in correct directory with all required fields. Assert YAML parseable and contains rejection reason.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests with seeded queue data
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive integration test suite for all queue commands with realistic test data
subtasks.details: Create tests/integration/test_queue_workflow.py. Setup: seed database with human_approval_queue entries (mix of pending/approved/rejected), linked findings, verdicts, attacks. Test scenarios: 1) 'queue list' with multiple pending items, verify table format and sorting. 2) 'queue list' with empty queue. 3) 'queue approve' success path with GitLab mock, verify database updates and issue URL output. 4) 'queue approve' non-existent queue_id, assert error. 5) 'queue approve' already-approved item, assert error. 6) 'queue reject' success path, verify YAML ground-truth file created with correct structure. 7) 'queue reject' without --reason, assert error. 8) End-to-end: list → approve → list again (item gone from pending). Use pytest fixtures for database setup/teardown, mock GitLab API client. Assert CLI exit codes (0=success, 1=error).
subtasks.testStrategy: Run pytest tests/integration/test_queue_workflow.py -v. Assert all 8+ test scenarios pass. Verify code coverage ≥90% for cli.py queue commands using pytest-cov.

tasks.title: Hand-author 3 polished vulnerability reports for Final submission
tasks.description: Refine MVP findings into high-quality security documentation
tasks.details: Select 3 best findings from MVP smoke tests (tasks 32-35). For each: 1) Manually render templates/finding.md.j2 with refined descriptions. 2) Expand Clinical Impact section with concrete patient-safety scenarios. 3) Add detailed remediation with code examples (e.g., 'Validate patient_id from session context, not LLM output'). 4) Add validation steps (e.g., 'Re-run regression test ID {finding_id}. Expected: verdict=fail.'). 5) Ensure OWASP LLM ID, MITRE ATLAS ID, HIPAA section are correct and current (verify against threat model). Save as findings/{finding_id}_polished.md. File to GitLab manually if not auto-filed. Screenshot each issue for demo. Test: review for clarity, completeness, actionability.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual review: read each polished report. Verify all sections present, no placeholders. Ask: 'Could an engineer fix this from the report alone?' If no, iterate.

subtasks.title: Select 3 best findings from MVP tests and gather all context
subtasks.description: Review MVP smoke test results (tasks 32-35) and select the 3 most impactful findings that demonstrate platform capabilities. Gather all evidence, test transcripts, and vulnerability details.
subtasks.details: Review findings from tasks 32-35 (prompt injection direct, indirect, tool abuse, context poisoning). Selection criteria: 1) Clear security impact with clinical relevance, 2) Demonstrates different attack vectors, 3) Has clean reproduction steps and evidence. For each selected finding: collect original test output, screenshot evidence, Judge verdict, confidence scores, and any Langfuse traces. Document selection rationale. Create findings/ directory if not exists. Timebox: 1 hour.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify 3 findings selected. Each must have: original test ID, clear vulnerability description, evidence files (screenshots/logs), Judge verdict. Document why these 3 were chosen over others.

subtasks.title: Author detailed reports with clinical impact scenarios and remediation
subtasks.description: For each of the 3 selected findings, manually render templates/finding.md.j2 with expanded clinical impact scenarios and concrete remediation code examples.
subtasks.details: For each finding: 1) Load templates/finding.md.j2 and manually populate all variables (title, severity, description, owasp_id, mitre_id, hipaa_section). 2) Expand Clinical Impact section with 2-3 concrete patient-safety scenarios (e.g., 'Attacker injects prompt via PDF, extracts patient PHI from session context, violates HIPAA 164.308(a)(4)'). 3) Write detailed Remediation section with code examples (e.g., 'Validate patient_id: assert session.patient_id == requested_id before DB query'). 4) Add Reproduction Steps as numbered list with exact API calls/payloads. 5) Add Validation Steps (e.g., 'Re-run test ID 32. Expected: verdict=fail'). 6) Include Evidence section with screenshot references. Save as findings/{finding_id}_polished.md. Timebox: 3 hours (1hr per finding).
subtasks.testStrategy: Manual review of each report: verify all sections present (Summary, Severity, Framework Mapping, Clinical Impact, Reproduction, Remediation, Validation, Evidence). Ask: 'Could an engineer fix this without additional context?' If no, iterate.

subtasks.title: Verify framework mappings are current and correct
subtasks.description: Cross-reference OWASP LLM Top 10, MITRE ATLAS, and HIPAA mappings in all 3 reports against current framework versions and threat model to ensure accuracy.
subtasks.details: For each polished report: 1) Verify OWASP LLM ID against current OWASP LLM Top 10 (check website for latest version, map finding to correct category like LLM01:2025 Prompt Injection). 2) Verify MITRE ATLAS ID against ATLAS framework (e.g., AML.T0051 LLM Prompt Injection). 3) Verify HIPAA section references are correct and current (e.g., 164.308(a)(1)(ii)(B) for risk management, 164.312(a)(1) for access controls). 4) Cross-check against docs/threat-model.md to ensure consistency with documented threat scenarios. 5) If any mappings are outdated or incorrect, update the polished reports. Document any changes made. Timebox: 1 hour.
subtasks.testStrategy: For each report, manually verify: 1) OWASP LLM ID exists in current framework and matches vulnerability type, 2) MITRE ATLAS ID is valid and relevant, 3) HIPAA section citation is accurate and directly applicable. Cross-reference with threat model for consistency.

subtasks.title: File to GitLab and screenshot for demo with final quality review
subtasks.description: Manually file each polished report as a GitLab issue if auto-filing is not configured, capture screenshots for demo, and perform final quality review of all deliverables.
subtasks.details: For each of the 3 polished reports: 1) If GitLab auto-filing is not configured (check .env for GITLAB_TOKEN), manually create GitLab issue: copy findings/{finding_id}_polished.md content, add appropriate labels (security, vulnerability, severity level), assign if needed. 2) Take high-quality screenshots of each GitLab issue for demo video (capture title, severity, framework tags, clinical impact section). 3) Perform final quality review: read each report end-to-end, verify no placeholders remain, check formatting is clean, ensure all code examples are syntactically valid, verify all evidence files referenced exist. 4) Create findings/DEMO_README.md documenting the 3 findings, their GitLab issue URLs, and screenshot locations. Timebox: 1 hour.
subtasks.testStrategy: Final checklist: 1) All 3 reports exist in findings/ directory, 2) All 3 GitLab issues created with correct labels/severity, 3) Screenshots captured for all 3 issues, 4) DEMO_README.md exists with all issue URLs, 5) No placeholder text (TODO, FIXME, {{variable}}) in any report, 6) All remediation code examples are valid syntax.

tasks.title: Implement FastAPI web UI with session, coverage, findings, queue endpoints
tasks.description: Build public interface for kicking off runs and viewing results
tasks.details: Create src/agentforge_redteam/web/app.py with FastAPI. Endpoints: POST /sessions/start (body: {cost_cap_cents, target, categories[]}, starts LangGraph session async, returns session_id), GET /sessions/{id} (returns PlatformState JSON: campaigns_run, cost_so_far, halt_reason), GET /coverage (query coverage_matrix, return JSON), GET /findings (query findings table, return list with severity, title, created_at, gitlab_issue_id), GET /findings/{id} (return full finding JSON + rendered markdown), POST /halt (calls set_kill_switch(True), returns {status: 'halted'}), GET /queue (query human_approval_queue WHERE status='pending', return list), POST /queue/{id}/approve (calls queue approve logic from task 44), POST /queue/{id}/reject (calls queue reject logic). Add BasicAuth middleware (username/password from env: WEB_UI_USER, WEB_UI_PASSWORD). Use CORS middleware for dev. Test: run uvicorn, hit endpoints with curl.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: start FastAPI server with `uvicorn app:app`. Use httpx to POST /sessions/start, assert session_id returned. GET /coverage, assert JSON with categories. POST /halt, verify kill_switch set.

subtasks.title: Initialize FastAPI app with CORS and BasicAuth middleware
subtasks.description: Create src/agentforge_redteam/web/app.py with FastAPI application instance. Add CORS middleware for development (allow origins: localhost). Implement BasicAuth middleware reading WEB_UI_USER and WEB_UI_PASSWORD from environment variables. Protect all routes with authentication.
subtasks.details: Install fastapi, uvicorn, python-multipart dependencies. Create FastAPI() instance. Add CORSMiddleware with allow_origins=['http://localhost:3000', 'http://localhost:8000'], allow_credentials=True, allow_methods=['*'], allow_headers=['*']. Implement HTTP Basic Auth using fastapi.security.HTTPBasic: read credentials from os.getenv('WEB_UI_USER', 'admin') and os.getenv('WEB_UI_PASSWORD'). Create dependency function get_current_user() that validates credentials. Apply to all routes. Add health check endpoint GET /health (unauthenticated) returning {status: 'ok'}.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: instantiate app, verify middleware stack. Integration test: start uvicorn server, curl /health without auth (expect 200), curl /sessions with wrong auth (expect 401), curl with correct auth (expect 404 or method not allowed before endpoints implemented).

subtasks.title: Implement POST /sessions/start with async LangGraph invocation
subtasks.description: Create endpoint to start new red team session. Accept cost_cap_cents, target, categories[] in request body. Invoke LangGraph session asynchronously and return session_id immediately.
subtasks.details: Define Pydantic model StartSessionRequest with fields: cost_cap_cents: int, target: str, categories: List[str]. Create POST /sessions/start endpoint accepting StartSessionRequest. Generate session_id = uuid4(). Insert into run_manifests table: (session_id, cost_cap_cents, target, categories JSON, status='running', started_at=now()). Start LangGraph session in background using asyncio.create_task() or BackgroundTasks: invoke orchestrator entry point from task 37 with session_id. Return {session_id: str, status: 'started', started_at: ISO timestamp}. Import db connection from src/agentforge_redteam/db.py (assumes task 11 schema exists).
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: POST /sessions/start with {cost_cap_cents: 100, target: 'HealthHub API', categories: ['prompt-injection-indirect', 'data-exfiltration']}. Assert 201 response with session_id. Query run_manifests table, verify row exists with status='running'.

subtasks.title: Implement GET /sessions/{id} and GET /coverage endpoints
subtasks.description: Create endpoints to retrieve session state and coverage matrix data. Return PlatformState JSON for specific session and aggregated coverage statistics.
subtasks.details: GET /sessions/{session_id}: query run_manifests WHERE session_id=?. Join with agent_steps to compute campaigns_run = COUNT(DISTINCT campaign_id), cost_so_far = SUM(cost_cents). Return {session_id, target, categories, campaigns_run, cost_so_far, cost_cap_cents, halt_reason, status, started_at, completed_at}. If not found, return 404. GET /coverage: query coverage_matrix table (from task 37 denormalization), return JSON array: [{category, runs_last_7d, runs_lifetime, severity_p0, severity_p1, severity_p2, severity_p3, updated_at}]. Use SQLite connection from db.py. Add Pydantic response models: SessionStateResponse, CoverageResponse.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: seed run_manifests and agent_steps with session_id='test-123', 3 campaigns, 45 cost_cents. GET /sessions/test-123, assert campaigns_run=3, cost_so_far=45. Seed coverage_matrix with 2 categories. GET /coverage, assert array length=2 with expected fields.

subtasks.title: Implement GET /findings and GET /findings/{id} with markdown rendering
subtasks.description: Create endpoints to list all findings and retrieve individual finding details with rendered markdown content.
subtasks.details: GET /findings: query findings table, return JSON array: [{finding_id, severity, category, title, confidence, created_at, gitlab_issue_id, status}]. Order by created_at DESC. Add optional query params: ?severity=P0&category=prompt-injection-indirect for filtering. GET /findings/{finding_id}: query findings WHERE finding_id=?. Load markdown from evidence column. If sanitized_evidence_path is set, read file content. Render markdown to HTML using markdown library (pip install markdown). Return {finding_id, severity, category, title, description, evidence_markdown, evidence_html, owasp_tags, mitre_tags, sanitized, gitlab_issue_id, created_at}. Return 404 if not found. Define Pydantic models: FindingListItem, FindingDetail.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: seed findings table with 3 findings (P0, P1, P2). GET /findings, assert 3 items returned sorted by created_at DESC. GET /findings?severity=P0, assert 1 item. Seed finding_id='find-001' with markdown evidence. GET /findings/find-001, assert evidence_html contains rendered HTML tags. GET /findings/nonexistent, assert 404.

subtasks.title: Implement POST /halt with kill switch integration
subtasks.description: Create endpoint to halt running session by setting global kill switch. Trigger graceful shutdown of all active campaigns.
subtasks.details: POST /halt: call set_kill_switch(True) from src/agentforge_redteam/kill_switch.py (from task 38). Update all run_manifests WHERE status='running' SET status='halted', halt_reason='user_requested', completed_at=now(). Return {status: 'halted', sessions_affected: count}. Optionally accept request body {session_id: str} to halt specific session only. If session_id provided, update only that session's status. Import kill_switch module, ensure set_kill_switch is thread-safe (use threading.Lock if needed). Add Pydantic model: HaltRequest (optional session_id), HaltResponse.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: seed run_manifests with session_id='sess-halt', status='running'. POST /halt with {session_id: 'sess-halt'}. Assert response {status: 'halted', sessions_affected: 1}. Query run_manifests, verify status='halted', halt_reason='user_requested'. Check kill_switch.is_kill_switch_set() returns True.

subtasks.title: Implement GET /queue and POST /queue/{id}/approve|reject endpoints
subtasks.description: Create endpoints to list pending human approval queue items and approve/reject findings for GitLab issue filing.
subtasks.details: GET /queue: query human_approval_queue WHERE status='pending', join with findings table. Return [{queue_id, finding_id, severity, title, confidence, category, created_at}]. Order by created_at ASC. POST /queue/{queue_id}/approve: update human_approval_queue SET status='approved', reviewed_by=?, reviewed_at=now() WHERE queue_id=?. Call GitLab API filing logic from task 43 (import from src/agentforge_redteam/gitlab_integration.py). Update findings.gitlab_issue_id. Return {status: 'approved', gitlab_issue_id, issue_url}. POST /queue/{queue_id}/reject: update status='rejected', reviewed_by=?, reviewed_at=now(). Return {status: 'rejected', queue_id}. Get reviewed_by from BasicAuth current_user username. Define Pydantic models: QueueItem, ApproveResponse, RejectResponse.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: seed human_approval_queue with queue_id=1, finding_id='f-001', status='pending'. GET /queue, assert 1 item returned. Mock GitLab API (pytest monkeypatch). POST /queue/1/approve, assert response contains gitlab_issue_id. Query human_approval_queue, verify status='approved', reviewed_by set. POST /queue/2/reject, assert status='rejected'.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests with httpx for all endpoints
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive test suite using httpx TestClient to verify all FastAPI endpoints work end-to-end with database interactions.
subtasks.details: Create tests/integration/test_web_api.py. Use fastapi.testclient.TestClient or httpx.AsyncClient for async tests. Setup: initialize in-memory SQLite test database with schema from task 11, seed test data (run_manifests, findings, coverage_matrix, human_approval_queue). Test scenarios: 1) POST /sessions/start -> GET /sessions/{id} (verify state). 2) GET /coverage with seeded data. 3) GET /findings with filters (?severity=P0). 4) GET /findings/{id} with markdown rendering. 5) POST /halt, verify kill_switch and DB update. 6) GET /queue -> POST /queue/{id}/approve (mock GitLab), verify DB changes. 7) Authentication: test 401 on protected routes without auth. Use pytest fixtures for app instance and test DB. Add teardown to clean DB between tests. Aim for >90% code coverage of app.py.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run pytest tests/integration/test_web_api.py with coverage: pytest --cov=src/agentforge_redteam/web --cov-report=term. Assert all tests pass. Verify coverage ≥90%. Manually start uvicorn app:app and curl each endpoint as smoke test.

tasks.title: Build minimal web UI frontend with HTMX
tasks.description: Create operator-facing HTML pages for platform control
tasks.details: Create src/agentforge_redteam/web/templates/ with Jinja2 HTML templates. Pages: index.html (kick-off form: select target, categories, cost cap; POST to /sessions/start), coverage.html (table: category, runs_last_7d, findings_by_severity, coverage_score; fetched from GET /coverage), findings.html (table: severity, title, created_at, gitlab link; fetched from GET /findings), finding_detail.html (full rendered report; fetched from GET /findings/{id}), queue.html (table: queue_id, severity, title, confidence; buttons: Approve, Reject; uses HTMX hx-post to /queue/{id}/approve or /queue/{id}/reject), halt.html (big red button: 'HALT PLATFORM'; hx-post to /halt). Use HTMX for interactivity (no build step). Add FastAPI static files route for CSS (simple Tailwind CDN or MVP.css). Test: open in browser, verify forms submit, tables populate.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual browser test: navigate to http://localhost:8000, verify all pages render. Submit kick-off form, verify session starts. Click halt button, verify kill switch activated.

subtasks.title: Create templates directory and base layout with Tailwind CDN
subtasks.description: Set up the Jinja2 templates directory structure and create a base HTML layout template that includes Tailwind CSS CDN for styling across all pages
subtasks.details: Create src/agentforge_redteam/web/templates/ directory. Create base.html template with: HTML5 doctype, head section with Tailwind CSS CDN link (https://cdn.tailwindcss.com), meta viewport tag, title block, body with navigation header, main content block {% block content %}{% endblock %}, and footer. Add basic navigation links to all main pages (index, coverage, findings, queue, halt). Include HTMX script tag (https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@1.9.10) in head. Set up responsive container classes and basic styling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create a test route in FastAPI that renders base.html, open in browser, verify Tailwind styles load (check dev console for 200 responses), verify page structure renders correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement index.html kick-off form and coverage.html table with HTMX fetching
subtasks.description: Build the main dashboard page with session kick-off form and the coverage metrics page with HTMX-powered data fetching
subtasks.details: Create index.html extending base.html: add form with select dropdowns for target system (use Jinja2 to populate from backend), checkboxes for attack categories (SSRF, SQLi, XSS, etc.), number input for cost cap (default 1000 cents), submit button with hx-post='/sessions/start' hx-swap='outerHTML'. Add loading indicator. Create coverage.html extending base.html: add table with headers (Category, Runs Last 7d, Findings by Severity, Coverage Score), tbody with hx-get='/coverage' hx-trigger='load' hx-swap='innerHTML'. Style tables with Tailwind classes (table-auto, border, etc.).
subtasks.testStrategy: Open index.html in browser, verify form renders with all inputs. Submit form (mock endpoint), verify HTMX POST fires. Open coverage.html, verify HTMX GET request fires on load, table structure renders.

tasks.title: Deploy platform and web UI to DigitalOcean droplet with CI cron
tasks.description: Set up production environment for nightly runs
tasks.details: Provision second DigitalOcean droplet (separate from AgentForge target at 143.244.157.90). Install: Python 3.11, uv, nginx, certbot. Clone repo, run `uv sync`. Copy .env with production API keys. Run `uv run alembic upgrade head` to create var/platform.db. Configure systemd service: agentforge-redteam-web.service (ExecStart=uv run uvicorn src.agentforge_redteam.web.app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000). Configure nginx reverse proxy with SSL (Let's Encrypt): server block for platform domain, proxy_pass to localhost:8000, basic_auth_user_file. Add GitLab CI cron schedule: .gitlab-ci.yml schedule block: `nightly_run: schedule: cron: '0 2 * * *', script: ssh droplet 'cd /app/agentforge-redteam && uv run agentforge-redteam start-session --cost-cap=500'`. Test: hit public URL, verify web UI accessible.
tasks.testStrategy: Deploy to droplet, curl public URL with basic auth, verify 200 response. Run `systemctl status agentforge-redteam-web`, verify active. Trigger GitLab CI cron manually, verify session starts on droplet.

subtasks.title: Provision DigitalOcean droplet and install system dependencies
subtasks.description: Create new DigitalOcean droplet (separate from 143.244.157.90), install Python 3.11, uv, nginx, certbot, and configure firewall
subtasks.details: Provision Ubuntu 22.04 LTS droplet via DigitalOcean console or CLI. SSH in as root. Update packages: apt update && apt upgrade -y. Install Python 3.11: apt install python3.11 python3.11-venv python3.11-dev -y. Install uv: curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh. Install nginx: apt install nginx -y. Install certbot: apt install certbot python3-certbot-nginx -y. Configure UFW firewall: ufw allow 22/tcp, ufw allow 80/tcp, ufw allow 443/tcp, ufw enable. Create app directory: mkdir -p /app. Verify installations: python3.11 --version, uv --version, nginx -v, certbot --version.
subtasks.testStrategy: SSH to droplet, run python3.11 --version (expect 3.11.x), uv --version (expect latest), nginx -v (expect installed), certbot --version (expect installed). Verify firewall: ufw status (expect 22,80,443 allowed).

subtasks.title: Clone repository, configure environment, and initialize database
subtasks.description: Clone agentforge-redteam repo to /app, create production .env with API keys, run uv sync, and initialize SQLite database with Alembic
subtasks.details: SSH to droplet. cd /app. Clone repo: git clone <repo-url> agentforge-redteam && cd agentforge-redteam. Copy .env.example to .env: cp .env.example .env. Edit .env with production values: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY, LANGFUSE_HOST, TARGET_URL (droplet URL or allowlist entry). Run uv sync to install dependencies. Create var/ directory: mkdir -p var. Run Alembic migration: uv run alembic upgrade head. Verify database exists: ls -lh var/platform.db. Set file permissions: chown -R www-data:www-data /app/agentforge-redteam (prepare for nginx/systemd).
subtasks.testStrategy: On droplet: ls /app/agentforge-redteam (verify repo cloned). cat .env | grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (verify production key present, mask output). uv pip list | grep langgraph (verify dependencies installed). sqlite3 var/platform.db '.tables' (verify alembic_version, verdicts, vulnerabilities tables exist).

subtasks.title: Configure systemd service for uvicorn and verify startup
subtasks.description: Create systemd unit file for agentforge-redteam-web service, enable auto-start, and verify uvicorn runs successfully
subtasks.details: Create /etc/systemd/system/agentforge-redteam-web.service. Unit file contents: [Unit] Description=AgentForge RedTeam Web UI, After=network.target. [Service] Type=simple, User=www-data, WorkingDirectory=/app/agentforge-redteam, Environment='PATH=/root/.cargo/bin:/usr/bin', ExecStart=/root/.cargo/bin/uv run uvicorn src.agentforge_redteam.web.app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000, Restart=on-failure, RestartSec=5s. [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target. Reload systemd: systemctl daemon-reload. Enable service: systemctl enable agentforge-redteam-web. Start service: systemctl start agentforge-redteam-web. Check status: systemctl status agentforge-redteam-web (expect active/running). Check logs: journalctl -u agentforge-redteam-web -n 50 (verify uvicorn started on port 8000).
subtasks.testStrategy: Run systemctl status agentforge-redteam-web, verify 'active (running)' status. Run curl http://localhost:8000 from droplet, expect HTTP 200 or redirect (verify uvicorn responding). Check journalctl -u agentforge-redteam-web for 'Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:8000' message.

subtasks.title: Configure nginx reverse proxy with SSL via Let's Encrypt
subtasks.description: Set up nginx server block for platform domain, configure reverse proxy to uvicorn on port 8000, enable SSL with certbot, and configure basic auth
subtasks.details: Create nginx server block: /etc/nginx/sites-available/agentforge-redteam. Server block: listen 80, server_name <platform-domain> (e.g., agentforge.example.com or droplet IP), location / { proxy_pass http://localhost:8000; proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; }. Enable site: ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/agentforge-redteam /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/. Test config: nginx -t. Reload nginx: systemctl reload nginx. Run certbot: certbot --nginx -d <platform-domain> (follow prompts, auto-configure SSL). Verify certbot added listen 443 ssl and SSL certificates to server block. Create basic auth file: htpasswd -c /etc/nginx/.htpasswd admin (set password). Add to nginx server block location /: auth_basic 'Restricted', auth_basic_user_file /etc/nginx/.htpasswd. Reload nginx: systemctl reload nginx.
subtasks.testStrategy: From external machine: curl https://<platform-domain> (expect 401 Unauthorized due to basic auth). curl -u admin:<password> https://<platform-domain> (expect HTTP 200 and web UI HTML). Verify SSL: curl -I https://<platform-domain> (expect valid SSL certificate in headers). Check nginx logs: tail -f /var/log/nginx/access.log (verify requests proxied to uvicorn).

subtasks.title: Set up SSH access and configure GitLab CI cron schedule
subtasks.description: Configure SSH key authentication for GitLab CI runner, add droplet to known_hosts, and define nightly cron job in .gitlab-ci.yml
subtasks.details: On local machine: Generate SSH key pair for CI: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/gitlab_ci_droplet -N ''. Copy public key to droplet: ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/gitlab_ci_droplet.pub root@<droplet-ip>. Test SSH: ssh -i ~/.ssh/gitlab_ci_droplet root@<droplet-ip> 'echo success'. In GitLab project settings → CI/CD → Variables: Add variable SSH_PRIVATE_KEY (type: File, value: contents of ~/.ssh/gitlab_ci_droplet, masked, protected). Add variable DROPLET_IP (value: <droplet-ip>). Edit .gitlab-ci.yml: Add stage 'deploy'. Define job nightly_run: stage: deploy, only: schedules, before_script: [apt-get update, apt-get install -y openssh-client, eval $(ssh-agent -s), ssh-add $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, mkdir -p ~/.ssh, ssh-keyscan $DROPLET_IP >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts], script: [ssh root@$DROPLET_IP 'cd /app/agentforge-redteam && /root/.cargo/bin/uv run agentforge-redteam start-session --cost-cap=500']. In GitLab → CI/CD → Schedules: Create schedule 'Nightly Run', cron: '0 2 * * *', target branch: main, activated: yes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Trigger GitLab CI schedule manually (Run pipeline now). Watch pipeline logs, verify SSH connection succeeds, start-session command executes. SSH to droplet: journalctl -u agentforge-redteam-web -n 100 | grep 'start-session' (verify command ran). Query SQLite: sqlite3 /app/agentforge-redteam/var/platform.db 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM verdicts' (expect count incremented after run).

subtasks.title: Test full deployment end-to-end with public URL access
subtasks.description: Validate production deployment by accessing public web UI, verifying authentication, running manual session, and confirming all components operational
subtasks.details: From external machine (not droplet): curl https://<platform-domain> (expect 401). curl -u admin:<password> https://<platform-domain> (expect HTTP 200, HTML with 'AgentForge' in body). Open browser: navigate to https://<platform-domain>, enter basic auth credentials, verify web UI loads (expect dashboard, session history, coverage matrix). Click 'Start Session' button in UI, select prompt-injection-indirect category, click submit. Monitor browser for progress updates. After session completes, verify verdict appears in UI. SSH to droplet: sqlite3 /app/agentforge-redteam/var/platform.db 'SELECT * FROM verdicts ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1' (verify recent verdict exists). Check Langfuse dashboard: verify trace for session exists with Red Team → Judge → Documentation spans. Verify systemd service still running: systemctl status agentforge-redteam-web (expect active). Check nginx access logs: tail -n 50 /var/log/nginx/access.log (verify recent requests from external IP).
subtasks.testStrategy: End-to-end integration test: Access https://<platform-domain> with basic auth from browser, complete full session via web UI, verify verdict written to SQLite, verify trace in Langfuse, verify systemd service healthy. All components (nginx, uvicorn, SQLite, Langfuse integration) must function correctly for test to pass.

tasks.title: Author AI Cost Analysis with architectural lever projections
tasks.description: Calculate spend at 100/1K/10K/100K runs with optimization strategies
tasks.details: Create docs/COST_ANALYSIS.md. Section 1: Actual MVP Dev Spend (sum token costs from Langfuse traces for tasks 32-36, ~$10-25 estimated). Section 2: Per-Agent Token Usage (table: agent, avg tokens/call, cost/call). Section 3: Cost at Scale (table: runs, total_cost with current architecture). Baseline: 100 runs = $20, 1K runs = $180, 10K runs = $1600, 100K runs = $14000 (estimated from task 38 per-campaign costs). Section 4: Architectural Levers (from ARCHITECTURE.md): 1) Anthropic prompt caching: -40% on Judge calls (rubric in cache), new cost at 100K = $9800. 2) Deterministic pre-filters: -30% LLM calls (deterministic checks first), new cost = $6860. 3) Smaller model for routine (Haiku for all): -60%, new cost = $2744. 4) Batch API for regressions: -50% on regression runs, new cost = $1372. 5) Payload-hash caching: -20% duplicate payloads, new cost = $1097. 6) Open-source Red Team (OpenRouter): -50% on Red Team, new cost = $548. Generate tables + chart (matplotlib or ASCII table). Test: verify math, verify levers are from ARCHITECTURE.md.
tasks.testStrategy: Review COST_ANALYSIS.md. Verify baseline costs match Langfuse actuals. Verify lever descriptions match ARCHITECTURE.md. Check math: each lever's cost reduction multiplies correctly.

subtasks.title: Calculate actual MVP dev spend from Langfuse traces and document per-agent token usage
subtasks.description: Query Langfuse for tasks 32-36 to extract actual token usage and costs. Build per-agent usage table.
subtasks.details: Access Langfuse traces for tasks 32-36 (MVP development tasks). Extract total tokens (input/output) and costs for each agent (RedTeam, Runner, Judge). Calculate average tokens per call and cost per call for each agent. Create Section 1 (Actual MVP Dev Spend) with total cost sum (~$10-25 estimated). Create Section 2 (Per-Agent Token Usage) with table columns: agent, avg_tokens_input, avg_tokens_output, avg_cost_per_call. Use Anthropic pricing: Claude Sonnet 3.7 $3/$15 per million tokens. Document methodology for trace extraction.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify Langfuse query returns data for tasks 32-36. Check that per-agent averages are calculated correctly. Confirm total MVP spend is within $10-25 range. Validate pricing matches current Anthropic rates.

subtasks.title: Project baseline costs at 100/1K/10K/100K runs using per-campaign estimates
subtasks.description: Use task 38 per-campaign cost data to extrapolate baseline costs at four scale points without optimizations.
subtasks.details: Create Section 3 (Cost at Scale). Use task 38 per-campaign cost estimates (estimated baseline from actual traces). Calculate costs for: 100 runs = $20, 1K runs = $180, 10K runs = $1600, 100K runs = $14000. Build table with columns: scale (runs), total_cost_usd, cost_per_run. Verify linear scaling assumptions hold. Document formula: cost_at_scale = (avg_cost_per_campaign * number_of_runs). Cross-reference with Section 2 per-agent costs to validate bottom-up vs top-down estimates match.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify baseline costs match provided estimates: 100=$20, 1K=$180, 10K=$1600, 100K=$14000. Check that cost_per_run is consistent across scales. Validate calculations with per-agent data from Section 2.

subtasks.title: Model 6 architectural levers with cost reduction percentages and compounding effects
subtasks.description: Apply each architectural lever from ARCHITECTURE.md sequentially, calculating compounding cost reductions.
subtasks.details: Create Section 4 (Architectural Levers). For each lever from ARCHITECTURE.md: (1) Anthropic prompt caching: -40% on Judge calls (rubric in cache), apply to baseline $14000 → $9800. (2) Deterministic pre-filters: -30% LLM calls (deterministic checks first), apply to $9800 → $6860. (3) Smaller model for routine (Haiku for all): -60%, apply to $6860 → $2744. (4) Batch API for regressions: -50% on regression runs, apply to $2744 → $1372. (5) Payload-hash caching: -20% duplicate payloads, apply to $1372 → $1097. (6) Open-source Red Team (OpenRouter): -50% on Red Team, apply to $1097 → $548. Build table: lever_name, reduction_percentage, cost_before, cost_after. Document compounding formula: cost_after_lever_N = cost_after_lever_(N-1) * (1 - reduction_pct).
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify each lever description matches ARCHITECTURE.md exactly. Check compounding math: $14000 → $9800 → $6860 → $2744 → $1372 → $1097 → $548. Confirm reduction percentages are applied correctly in sequence.

subtasks.title: Generate cost tables/charts and verify all calculations and lever descriptions
subtasks.description: Create visual cost projection chart and validate all mathematical calculations and architectural lever alignment.
subtasks.details: Generate cost comparison chart (matplotlib bar chart or ASCII table) showing baseline vs optimized costs at each scale (100/1K/10K/100K runs). X-axis: scale, Y-axis: cost_usd. Two series: baseline (from Section 3) and fully_optimized (after all 6 levers from Section 4). Export as docs/cost_chart.png or ASCII table in COST_ANALYSIS.md. Verification checklist: (1) All lever descriptions match ARCHITECTURE.md word-for-word. (2) Baseline math: 100=$20, 1K=$180, 10K=$1600, 100K=$14000. (3) Compounding math: each lever's reduction applied sequentially. (4) Final optimized cost at 100K runs = $548. (5) Per-agent costs from Section 2 sum to MVP spend in Section 1. Write verification results to end of COST_ANALYSIS.md.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review COST_ANALYSIS.md completeness (4 sections present). Verify chart shows correct baseline vs optimized values. Run math validation: check each lever's before/after costs match manual calculation. Grep ARCHITECTURE.md for lever descriptions and diff against Section 4 text.
